name: RABBIT-CAGE-BACKLOG-28
title: Housekeeping wave 1: Inv 53 reconcile + version 4.7.0 + dead-constants + hook _log_exc dedup + scope-guard ergonomics + CLI consistency
description:
Bundled housekeeping items for rabbit-cage, surfaced by wave-1 review.

MEDIUM:
(M3) Spec Inv 53 says rabbit-config SKILL.md description names 'all six subcommands' but the description actually names seven (help, prompt-threshold, allowed-tools, bash-allow, permissions, human-approval, bypass-permissions). Inv 82 codified the 'help' subcommand later. Inv 53 not reconciled. Update Inv 53 wording: 'all seven subcommands'.

(M4) PR #145 and PR #150 both shipped under version 4.6.0 (additive Inv 88/89 should have been 4.7.0). PR #151 (banner BYPASS-PERMISSIONS) also under 4.6.0. Bump to 4.7.0 (or 4.8.0 if 4.7.0 conflicts) across feature.json + spec.md + contract.md frontmatter — three-way alignment per Inv 84. Note this collision in the bump commit message.

LOW:
(F3) hooks/_runtime_flags.py exports BYPASS_PERMISSIONS_BODY + BYPASS_PERMISSIONS_REVOKE + HUMAN_APPROVAL_BODY + HUMAN_APPROVAL_REVOKE as module-level names. After PR #151 swap, sync-check.py no longer imports BYPASS_PERMISSIONS_BODY. session-init.py imports active_flags() only. Make the body/revoke constants private (_BYPASS_BODY etc.) or drop them — test file redefines locally anyway.

(F4) sync-check.py imports rabbit_subline from rabbit_print but never uses it (only rabbit_block + named wrappers are called). Drop the unused import.

(F5) hooks/sync-check.py and hooks/session-init.py duplicate _log_exc() verbatim. Lift into _runtime_flags.py (or a new _hook_helpers.py) as _log_exc(script_tag, where, exc). Inv 70 currently scopes _log_exc to those two hooks; centralizing eliminates duplication.

(F6) test-backlog-27-runtime-flags-display.py (361 LOC) redefines BYPASS_PERMISSIONS_BODY + HUMAN_APPROVAL_BODY as local module constants instead of importing from _runtime_flags.py — guaranteed silent drift if canonical text changes. Import from _runtime_flags or assert equality with helper exports.

(F7) Spec invariant numbering ordering drift (separate from BACKLOG-25 which is duplicates): Inv 86/87 appear inline among Inv 53-60; Inv 88/89 sit between Inv 59 and 60. Numerically out-of-order blocks. Different from BACKLOG-25. Consider a one-pass renumber + a test-spec-invariant-numbering.py asserting monotonic order. Lower priority — flagged for awareness.

(F8) hooks/scope-guard.py decide() default-deny message lists 3 options but doesn't name override-revoke mechanism. After session override granted, operators must discover scope-guard-on.py separately. Add: '(4) REVOKE SESSION OVERRIDE — run .claude/features/rabbit-cage/scripts/scope-guard-on.py' or fold one-liner into option (1).

(F9) rabbit-config.py cmd_permissions:233-240 error path emits bare 'Error: permissions requires lock or unlock' without USAGE block. Other subcommands include 'expected ...' and/or full USAGE on error. Align.

Acceptance: Inv 53 reconciled, version bumped + three-way aligned, dead exports removed, dead import removed, _log_exc deduplicated, scope-guard message extended, CLI error message aligned, test imports canonical strings. Note: F7 (numbering ordering) is acknowledged but may be deferred if scope grows too large — work on best effort.
